3	// execute_bead_land.go — the land coordinator pattern.
4	//
5	// This file implements the human-PR landing model for execute-bead results.
6	// The old Merge() path in execute_bead_orchestrator.go has been deleted; all
7	// target-ref writes now flow through Land(), which is called exactly once per
8	// submission by a per-project coordinator goroutine (see
9	// internal/server/workers.go:LandCoordinator).
10	//
11	// The flow mirrors how a human merges PRs:
12	//
13	//   1. Fetch the current target tip (from origin when a remote exists, or
14	//      from the local branch otherwise).
15	//   2. If the current tip equals the worker's BaseRev — fast-forward the
16	//      target branch directly to the worker's ResultRev via update-ref. The
17	//      worker's commit keeps its original parent; no new commit is created.
18	//   3. Otherwise — the target has advanced since the worker started. Create
19	//      a temporary detached worktree at the current target tip, run
20	//      `git merge --no-ff` to introduce the worker's ResultRev, and
21	//      fast-forward the target branch to the resulting merge commit. The
22	//      worker's commit is NEVER rewritten: its parent is still BaseRev, so
23	//      a later replay observes the same inputs the worker saw.
24	//   4. If the merge conflicts — abort cleanly, preserve the original
25	//      ResultRev under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>,
26	//      and return preserved status. Target branch is never modified.
27	//   5. If an origin remote exists — push the new target tip. The push is
28	//      strictly fast-forward; push failures are reported via PushFailed but
29	//      do not roll back the local target ref.
30	//
31	// Replay fidelity is the reason for merge-over-rebase: a rebased commit has
32	// a rewritten parent that lies about what the worker saw at execution time.
33	// A merge commit preserves both histories — the worker's original commit
34	// (parent = BaseRev) and the target's prior tip — losslessly.
35	//
36	// The coordinator owning the goroutine provides the serialization guarantee,
37	// so Land() itself does not take any locks.
38

40		"context"
41		"fmt"
42		"os"
43		"path/filepath"
44		"strings"
45	
46		internalgit "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
47	)

49	// LandRequest is one submission to the land coordinator: "here is the worker's
50	// result from base B to result R for bead X; land it on the project's target
51	// branch."
52	type LandRequest struct {
53		// WorktreeDir is the path to the project's repository directory (the
54		// directory the coordinator operates on). The original worker worktree
55		// has typically already been removed by the time Land() runs — Land()
56		// creates its own temporary worktrees when a merge is needed.
57		WorktreeDir string
58	
59		// BaseRev is the revision the worker branched off when it started.
60		// When the current target tip equals BaseRev, Land() takes the fast path.
61		BaseRev string
62	
63		// ResultRev is the worker's final commit SHA. Must be reachable in the
64		// project's git object store at the time Land() is called.
65		ResultRev string
66	
67		// BeadID identifies the bead this submission is for. Used to build the
68		// preserve-ref path on conflict.
69		BeadID string
70	
71		// AttemptID uniquely identifies this land attempt. Used to build the
72		// preserve-ref path on conflict so concurrent attempts for the same
73		// bead do not collide.
74		AttemptID string
75	
76		// TargetBranch is the branch to advance. When empty, Land() resolves
77		// the project's current HEAD branch and uses that.
78		TargetBranch string
79	
80		// EvidenceDir is the relative path to the per-attempt execution evidence
81		// directory (e.g. ".ddx/executions/20260416T181205-b5419982"). When
82		// non-empty and the main land succeeds, Land() creates a trailing
83		// evidence commit that folds these files into the target branch. The
84		// agent's commit SHA is NOT amended — the evidence commit is a separate
85		// child commit, preserving closing_commit_sha references.
86		EvidenceDir string
87	}
88	
89	// LandResult describes the outcome of a single Land() call.
90	type LandResult struct {
91		// Status is one of:
92		//   - "landed":    the target branch now points at a new commit
93		//                  (either ResultRev itself on the fast-forward path,
94		//                  or a merge commit on the merge path).
95		//   - "preserved": the merge conflicted; ResultRev is saved under
96		//                  PreserveRef and the target branch is unchanged.
97		//   - "no-changes": ResultRev == BaseRev; nothing to land.
98		Status string
99	
100		// NewTip is the new value of the target branch when Status == "landed".
101		// On the fast-forward path NewTip == ResultRev; on the merge path NewTip
102		// is the SHA of the merge commit (whose parents are the prior target
103		// tip and ResultRev). Empty when preserved or no-changes.
104		NewTip string
105	
106		// Merged is true when the land took the merge-commit path (current tip
107		// had advanced beyond BaseRev, so Land() created a merge commit to
108		// combine the worker's result with the new target tip). False on the
109		// fast-forward path where the worker's commit became the new tip
110		// unchanged.
111		Merged bool
112	
113		// PreserveRef is set when Status == "preserved". It names the ref under
114		// refs/ddx/iterations/ where ResultRev was saved for later recovery.
115		PreserveRef string
116	
117		// Reason is a human-readable explanation, especially useful when
118		// Status == "preserved" (e.g. "merge conflict") or when PushFailed.
119		Reason string
120	
121		// PushFailed is true when the local target ref was advanced successfully
122		// but the subsequent push to origin was rejected (e.g. non-fast-forward).
123		// The local state is authoritative; the remote will need to be
124		// reconciled by a later land or an operator.
125		PushFailed bool
126	
127		// PushError captures the underlying push error when PushFailed is true.
128		PushError string
129	
130		// PushConflict is true when the initial push was rejected (e.g. origin
131		// advanced) and the loop's automatic pull/merge/retry recovery ran into
132		// a merge conflict it cannot resolve without operator input. Distinct
133		// from PushFailed so the loop can park the bead for human review under
134		// a short cooldown instead of treating it as a generic push failure.
135		PushConflict bool
136	
137		// PushRecovered is true when the initial push was rejected but the loop
138		// successfully fetched, merged, and re-pushed without operator input.
139		// The land is fully complete; PushFailed and PushConflict are false.
140		// Set so callers can record telemetry on how often the auto-recovery
141		// path actually saves an operator round-trip.
142		PushRecovered bool
143	
144		// MergedCommitCount is the number of commits reachable from ResultRev but
145		// not from BaseRev — i.e. the "size" of the worker's contribution being
146		// merged in. Zero on the no-changes path. Set on both the fast-forward
147		// and merge-commit paths so metrics can compare contribution sizes.
148		MergedCommitCount int
149	
150		// EvidenceCommitSHA is set when a trailing execution-evidence commit was
151		// created after the main land. When set, NewTip points at this commit
152		// (not at the original agent commit or merge commit).
153		EvidenceCommitSHA string
154	}

156	// PreClaimResult is the outcome of a FetchOriginAncestryCheck call.
157	type PreClaimResult struct {
158		// Action is one of:
159		//   "unchanged"     — local tip == origin tip, no action taken
160		//   "fast-forwarded"— local was behind origin; local branch was advanced
161		//   "no-origin"     — no origin remote; check skipped
162		//   "local-ahead"   — local is ahead of origin; no action needed
163		//   "diverged"      — neither is ancestor of the other; claim should be aborted
164		Action    string
165		LocalSHA  string
166		OriginSHA string
167	}

169	// LandingGitOps abstracts the git operations Land() needs. RealLandingGitOps
170	// shells out to git; tests inject fakes or run against real temp repos.
171	type LandingGitOps interface {
172		// HasRemote reports whether the given remote name exists in dir.
173		HasRemote(dir, remote string) bool
174	
175		// CurrentBranch returns the branch HEAD currently points at in dir, or
176		// an error if HEAD is detached or unresolvable.
177		CurrentBranch(dir string) (string, error)
178	
179		// FetchBranch fetches remote/branch into dir (no merge, no checkout).
180		// Returns nil when no remote exists.
181		FetchBranch(dir, remote, branch string) error
182	
183		// ResolveRef resolves ref (e.g. "refs/heads/main" or "origin/main") to a
184		// commit SHA. When ref is unresolvable returns ("", error).
185		ResolveRef(dir, ref string) (string, error)
186	
187		// UpdateRefTo updates ref in dir to sha. When oldSHA is non-empty, the
188		// update is conditional on the current ref value equalling oldSHA.
189		UpdateRefTo(dir, ref, sha, oldSHA string) error
190	
191		// SyncWorkTreeToHead syncs the index AND the working-tree files in dir
192		// to HEAD after a non-checkout ref update (e.g. update-ref). fromRev is
193		// the commit HEAD pointed at BEFORE the ref update; it is used to
194		// compute the minimal set of tracked files changed by the update so
195		// that unrelated local modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat
196		// writes, operator scratch) are NOT clobbered.
197		//
198		// Needed because Land() advances the target ref via update-ref, which
199		// touches neither the index nor the worktree. Before this fix, Land()
200		// only ran `git read-tree HEAD` to sync the index — leaving the main
201		// worktree showing phantom deleted/modified entries for every file the
202		// landed commit touched, and subsequent agents reading files from disk
203		// would see stale content.
204		//
205		// Implementation: `git read-tree HEAD` + `git diff --name-only fromRev
206		// HEAD` to enumerate changed files + `git checkout-index -f --` to
207		// materialize them from the freshly-synced index.
208		SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev string) error
209	
210		// AddWorktree creates a new worktree at path checked out at rev in dir.
211		AddWorktree(dir, path, rev string) error
212	
213		// RemoveWorktree removes the worktree at path in dir (force).
214		RemoveWorktree(dir, path string) error
215	
216		// MergeInto runs `git merge --no-ff -m msg srcRev` inside wtDir, which
217		// must already be checked out at the current target tip. A clean merge
218		// produces a merge commit whose parents are [currentTip, srcRev]; the
219		// commit SHA can be read with HeadRevAt. Returns nil on clean merge,
220		// or an error on conflict. On error, the implementation is responsible
221		// for running `git merge --abort` so the worktree is left clean.
222		MergeInto(wtDir, srcRev, msg string) error
223	
224		// HeadRevAt returns HEAD of the git workdir at dir.
225		HeadRevAt(dir string) (string, error)
226	
227		// PushFFOnly pushes localRef to remote as targetBranch with strict
228		// fast-forward semantics. Returns an error when the push would not be
229		// fast-forward or when the network call fails.
230		PushFFOnly(dir, remote, localRef, targetBranch string) error
231	
232		// FetchOriginAncestryCheck fetches origin/targetBranch and compares it
233		// to the local branch tip. When origin is ahead the local branch is
234		// fast-forwarded via update-ref + read-tree. When the two tips have
235		// diverged (neither is ancestor of the other) the returned result has
236		// Action == "diverged". When no origin remote exists the result has
237		// Action == "no-origin". The caller decides whether to abort a claim.
238		FetchOriginAncestryCheck(dir, targetBranch string) (PreClaimResult, error)
239	
240		// CountCommits returns the number of commits reachable from tip but not
241		// from base (i.e. git rev-list --count base..tip). Used to record the
242		// contribution size in land metrics. Returns 0 on error.
243		CountCommits(dir, base, tip string) int
244	
245		// StageDir stages all files under relPath in dir for the next commit.
246		StageDir(dir, relPath string) error
247	
248		// CommitStaged creates a commit from currently staged changes using msg
249		// as the commit message. Returns (sha, nil) when a commit was made,
250		// ("", nil) when nothing was staged, and ("", err) on failure.
251		CommitStaged(dir, msg string) (string, error)
252	}

254	// RealLandingGitOps implements LandingGitOps via os/exec git commands.
255	type RealLandingGitOps struct{}
256	
257	func (RealLandingGitOps) HasRemote(dir, remote string) bool {
258		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "remote").Output()
259		if err != nil {
260			return false
261		}
262		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), "\n") {
263			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == remote {
264				return true
265			}
266		}
267		return false
268	}
269	
270	func (RealLandingGitOps) CurrentBranch(dir string) (string, error) {
271		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "symbolic-ref", "--short", "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
272		if err != nil {
273			return "", fmt.Errorf("git symbolic-ref HEAD: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
274		}
275		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
276	}
277	
278	func (RealLandingGitOps) FetchBranch(dir, remote, branch string) error {
279		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "fetch", remote, branch).CombinedOutput()
280		if err != nil {
281			return fmt.Errorf("git fetch %s %s: %s: %w", remote, branch, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
282		}
283		return nil
284	}
285	
286	func (RealLandingGitOps) ResolveRef(dir, ref string) (string, error) {
287		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", ref).CombinedOutput()
288		if err != nil {
289			return "", fmt.Errorf("git rev-parse %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
290		}
291		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
292	}
293	
294	func (RealLandingGitOps) UpdateRefTo(dir, ref, sha, oldSHA string) error {
295		args := []string{"update-ref", ref, sha}
296		if oldSHA != "" {
297			args = append(args, oldSHA)
298		}
299		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).CombinedOutput()
300		if err != nil {
301			return fmt.Errorf("git update-ref %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
302		}
303		return nil
304	}
305	
306	func (RealLandingGitOps) SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev string) error {
307		// Step 1: sync the index to HEAD. This is required before checkout-index
308		// below will do anything useful, and also keeps subsequent CommitTracker
309		// calls from building stale trees.
310		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "read-tree", "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
311		if err != nil {
312			return fmt.Errorf("git read-tree HEAD: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
313		}
314	
315		// Step 2: compute the list of tracked files changed between the previous
316		// HEAD and the current HEAD. These are the files that the landing commit
317		// added, modified, or deleted. We only restore THESE files to avoid
318		// clobbering unrelated local modifications (agent-logs being written by
319		// the running server, beads.jsonl heartbeat updates, operator scratch).
320		if fromRev == "" {
321			// No previous HEAD known — fall back to the unsafe behaviour of
322			// read-tree only. Acceptable when the caller is a best-effort path
323			// that cannot reconstruct fromRev.
324			return nil
325		}
326		diffOut, diffErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--name-only", fromRev, "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
327		if diffErr != nil {
328			// Diff failed (bad fromRev, shallow history, etc.) — leave the
329			// worktree stale rather than risk a broken checkout. The CommitTracker
330			// stale-tree bug is the prior status quo and no worse than before.
331			return nil
332		}
333		changed := strings.Fields(strings.TrimSpace(string(diffOut)))
334		if len(changed) == 0 {
335			return nil
336		}
337	
338		// Step 3: split into existing-in-HEAD (checkout-index) and
339		// deleted-in-HEAD (os.Remove) buckets. checkout-index only writes files
340		// that are in the index; it cannot represent a deletion, so we handle
341		// those ourselves.
342		var indexFiles []string
343		var removedFiles []string
344		for _, f := range changed {
345			probe := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "ls-files", "--error-unmatch", "--", f)
346			if probe.Run() == nil {
347				indexFiles = append(indexFiles, f)
348			} else {
349				removedFiles = append(removedFiles, f)
350			}
351		}
352	
353		// Step 4: materialize the index-present files into the working tree.
354		// -f overwrites any stale content at these exact paths. Unrelated files
355		// are untouched because we pass the specific path list.
356		if len(indexFiles) > 0 {
357			args := []string{"checkout-index", "-f", "--"}
358			args = append(args, indexFiles...)
359			out2, err2 := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).CombinedOutput()
360			if err2 != nil {
361				return fmt.Errorf("git checkout-index -f: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out2)), err2)
362			}
363		}
364	
365		// Step 5: remove files that the landing commit deleted and whose removal
366		// did not propagate to the worktree because update-ref bypassed the
367		// working-tree update.
368		for _, f := range removedFiles {
369			full := filepath.Join(dir, f)
370			_ = os.Remove(full) // best-effort; leave the file if removal fails
371		}
372	
373		return nil
374	}
375	
376	func (RealLandingGitOps) AddWorktree(dir, path, rev string) error {
377		// --detach so the worktree does not create a persistent branch.
378		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "add", "--force", "--detach", path, rev).CombinedOutput()
379		if err != nil {
380			return fmt.Errorf("git worktree add %s %s: %s: %w", path, rev, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
381		}
382		return nil
383	}
384	
385	func (RealLandingGitOps) RemoveWorktree(dir, path string) error {
386		_ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "remove", "--force", path).Run()
387		_ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "worktree", "prune").Run()
388		return nil
389	}

391	func (RealLandingGitOps) MergeInto(wtDir, srcRev, msg string) error {
392		// --no-ff forces a merge commit even when the merge could fast-forward
393		// (which shouldn't happen given our caller's ancestry check, but is a
394		// defensive guarantee that target history always gets a marker commit).
395		// We inject user.name/user.email via -c so the merge commit can be
396		// created even when the worktree inherited no git config; the
397		// coordinator is a machine actor and should not adopt a human's identity.
398		out, err := internalgit.Command(
399			context.Background(), wtDir,
400			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
401			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",
402			"merge", "--no-ff", "-m", msg, srcRev,
403		).CombinedOutput()
404		if err != nil {
405			_ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), wtDir, "merge", "--abort").Run()
406			return fmt.Errorf("git merge --no-ff %s: %s: %w", srcRev, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
407		}
408		return nil
409	}

421		refspec := fmt.Sprintf("%s:refs/heads/%s", localRef, targetBranch)
422		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "push", remote, refspec).CombinedOutput()

429	func (RealLandingGitOps) CountCommits(dir, base, tip string) int {
430		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-list", "--count", base+".."+tip).CombinedOutput()
431		if err != nil {
432			return 0
433		}
434		s := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
435		n := 0
436		for _, c := range s {
437			if c >= '0' && c <= '9' {
438				n = n*10 + int(c-'0')
439			}
440		}
441		return n
442	}

448		// (ddx-39e27896). manifest.json, result.json, prompt.md, and

461		if len(strings.TrimSpace(string(out))) == 0 {
462			return "", nil
463		}

465			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",

467			"commit", "-m", msg,

473		if err != nil {
474			return "", fmt.Errorf("rev-parse HEAD after evidence commit: %w", err)
475		}

479	// FetchOriginAncestryCheck implements LandingGitOps.FetchOriginAncestryCheck.
480	func (RealLandingGitOps) FetchOriginAncestryCheck(dir, targetBranch string) (PreClaimResult, error) {
481		// Step 1: check for origin remote.
482		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "remote", "get-url", "origin").CombinedOutput()
483		if err != nil {
484			// No origin remote — single-machine case; skip check.
485			return PreClaimResult{Action: "no-origin"}, nil
486		}
487		_ = out // remote URL not needed
488	
489		// Step 2: fetch origin/targetBranch (best-effort; failure is non-fatal but surfaced).
490		if fetchOut, fetchErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "fetch", "origin", targetBranch).CombinedOutput(); fetchErr != nil {
491			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("git fetch origin %s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(fetchOut)), fetchErr)
492		}
493	
494		// Step 3: resolve local tip.
495		localOut, localErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", "refs/heads/"+targetBranch).CombinedOutput()
496		if localErr != nil {
497			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("resolving local %s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(localOut)), localErr)
498		}
499		localSHA := strings.TrimSpace(string(localOut))
500	
501		// Step 4: resolve origin tip.
502		originOut, originErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", "refs/remotes/origin/"+targetBranch).CombinedOutput()
503		if originErr != nil {
504			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("resolving origin/%s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(originOut)), originErr)
505		}
506		originSHA := strings.TrimSpace(string(originOut))
507	
508		// Step 5: compare.
509		if localSHA == originSHA {
510			return PreClaimResult{Action: "unchanged", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
511		}
512	
513		// Is local an ancestor of origin? (origin is ahead)
514		localAncestorErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", localSHA, originSHA).Run()
515		if localAncestorErr == nil {
516			// Origin is ahead: fast-forward local branch via update-ref + sync worktree.
517			targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
518			if upErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "update-ref", targetRef, originSHA, localSHA).Run(); upErr != nil {
519				return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to %s: %w", targetRef, originSHA, upErr)
520			}
521			// Sync index + working tree to new HEAD so the main worktree files
522			// reflect the pulled-down origin commits. Pass localSHA as fromRev
523			// to restrict the restore to files actually changed by origin's
524			// advance, preserving unrelated local modifications.
525			_ = (RealLandingGitOps{}).SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, localSHA)
526			return PreClaimResult{Action: "fast-forwarded", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
527		}
528	
529		// Is origin an ancestor of local? (local is ahead)
530		originAncestorErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", originSHA, localSHA).Run()
531		if originAncestorErr == nil {
532			return PreClaimResult{Action: "local-ahead", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
533		}
534	
535		// Neither is ancestor of the other: diverged.
536		return PreClaimResult{Action: "diverged", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
537	}

539	// landIterationRef returns the documented hidden ref for a land-time preserve.
540	// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>. The short-tip
541	// captures the current target tip at the time of the conflict so subsequent
542	// recovery tools can reconstruct which merge target was in play.
543	func landIterationRef(beadID, attemptID, tip string) string {
544		short := tip
545		if len(short) > 12 {
546			short = short[:12]
547		}
548		attempt := attemptID
549		if attempt == "" {
550			attempt = NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z")
551		}
552		return fmt.Sprintf("refs/ddx/iterations/%s/%s-%s", beadID, attempt, short)
553	}
554	
555	// landEvidence creates a trailing commit that folds the per-attempt execution
556	// evidence directory into the target branch. Called after the main land (ff or
557	// merge) succeeds and before the push. The evidence commit is a normal child of
558	// result.NewTip — the agent's original commit SHA is not amended.
559	//
560	// Best-effort: only works when HEAD is a symbolic ref to targetBranch (the
561	// normal case for server workers and CLI users). When HEAD is detached or
562	// points elsewhere, the evidence commit is silently skipped.
563	func landEvidence(wd, targetBranch string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps, result *LandResult) {
564		branch, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
565		if err != nil || branch != targetBranch {
566			return
567		}
568		if err := gitOps.StageDir(wd, req.EvidenceDir); err != nil {
569			return
570		}
571		msg := fmt.Sprintf("chore: add execution evidence [%s]", shortAttempt(req.AttemptID))
572		sha, err := gitOps.CommitStaged(wd, msg)
573		if err != nil || sha == "" {
574			return
575		}
576		result.EvidenceCommitSHA = sha
577		result.NewTip = sha
578	}
579	
580	// Land performs fetch → (ff or merge) → push for a single submission.
581	// Callers MUST serialize calls per projectRoot (the server coordinator
582	// goroutine provides this). Land() itself takes no internal locks.
583	//
584	// projectRoot is the directory containing the project's .git. req.WorktreeDir,
585	// when non-empty, is used as the git working directory for all commands; when
586	// empty, projectRoot is used. Both usually refer to the same dir — the field
587	// is kept for forward compatibility with multi-clone topologies.
588	func Land(projectRoot string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps) (*LandResult, error) {
589		if gitOps == nil {
590			gitOps = RealLandingGitOps{}
591		}
592		wd := req.WorktreeDir
593		if wd == "" {
594			wd = projectRoot
595		}
596	
597		// Trivial guard — no commits to land.
598		if req.ResultRev == "" || req.ResultRev == req.BaseRev {
599			return &LandResult{Status: "no-changes", Reason: "result_rev == base_rev"}, nil
600		}
601	
602		// Resolve target branch (default to project HEAD).
603		targetBranch := req.TargetBranch
604		if targetBranch == "" {
605			br, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
606			if err != nil {
607				return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving target branch: %w", err)
608			}
609			targetBranch = br
610		}
611		targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
612	
613		// Fetch the current target tip from origin when available; otherwise
614		// read the local branch directly.
615		hasOrigin := gitOps.HasRemote(wd, "origin")
616		if hasOrigin {
617			// Fetch is best-effort: a disconnected remote must not block
618			// a local land. Log-style errors are swallowed here; the
619			// subsequent ResolveRef will surface any fatal issue.
620			_ = gitOps.FetchBranch(wd, "origin", targetBranch)
621		}
622		currentTip, err := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, targetRef)
623		if err != nil {
624			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving target tip %s: %w", targetRef, err)
625		}
626	
627		contribCount := gitOps.CountCommits(wd, req.BaseRev, req.ResultRev)
628	
629		// Fast path: no sibling advanced the target branch → straight ff via
630		// update-ref. The worker's commit becomes the new tip unchanged; its
631		// parent is still BaseRev, so replay sees the same inputs the worker
632		// saw. No merge commit is created.
633		if currentTip == req.BaseRev {
634			if err := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, req.ResultRev, currentTip); err != nil {
635				return nil, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to %s: %w", targetRef, req.ResultRev, err)
636			}
637			// Refresh the main worktree's index + working-tree files so the
638			// operator and subsequent agents see the newly-landed commit's
639			// changes. Pass currentTip as fromRev so only files actually
640			// changed by this commit are restored — unrelated local
641			// modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat) are preserved.
642			// Non-fatal on error.
643			_ = gitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead(wd, currentTip)
644			result := &LandResult{
645				Status:            "landed",
646				NewTip:            req.ResultRev,
647				Merged:            false,
648				MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
649			}
650			if req.EvidenceDir != "" {
651				landEvidence(wd, targetBranch, req, gitOps, result)
652			}
653			landPush(wd, targetBranch, result.NewTip, hasOrigin, gitOps, result)
654			return result, nil
655		}
656	
657		// Merge path: the target has advanced since the worker started. Create
658		// a temp detached worktree at currentTip and run `git merge --no-ff
659		// ResultRev` inside it. The result is a merge commit whose parents are
660		// [currentTip, ResultRev]. Crucially, ResultRev itself is NOT rewritten:
661		// its parent is still BaseRev, so replay observes the original inputs.
662		tempWT, tempWtErr := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-land-wt-*")
663		if tempWtErr != nil {
664			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating temp worktree dir: %w", tempWtErr)
665		}
666		// os.MkdirTemp creates the dir, but git worktree add refuses to run if
667		// the target already exists. Remove it first so git can recreate it.
668		_ = os.RemoveAll(tempWT)
669		if err := gitOps.AddWorktree(wd, tempWT, currentTip); err != nil {
670			return nil, fmt.Errorf("adding temp worktree at %s: %w", currentTip, err)
671		}
672		defer func() { _ = gitOps.RemoveWorktree(wd, tempWT) }()
673	
674		mergeMsg := fmt.Sprintf("Merge bead %s attempt %s into %s", req.BeadID, shortAttempt(req.AttemptID), targetBranch)
675		if err := gitOps.MergeInto(tempWT, req.ResultRev, mergeMsg); err != nil {
676			// Merge conflict: preserve the original ResultRev and return.
677			preserveRef := landIterationRef(req.BeadID, req.AttemptID, currentTip)
678			if upErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, preserveRef, req.ResultRev, ""); upErr != nil {
679				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving %s after merge conflict: %w", preserveRef, upErr)
680			}
681			return &LandResult{
682				Status:            "preserved",
683				PreserveRef:       preserveRef,
684				Reason:            "merge conflict",
685				MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
686			}, nil
687		}
688	
689		// Merge clean: read the merge commit SHA from the temp worktree's HEAD
690		// and fast-forward the target branch to it.
691		mergeSHA, err := gitOps.HeadRevAt(tempWT)
692		if err != nil {
693			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading merge HEAD: %w", err)
694		}
695		if err := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, mergeSHA, currentTip); err != nil {
696			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to merge commit %s: %w", targetRef, mergeSHA, err)
697		}
698		// Refresh the main worktree's index + working-tree files. Pass
699		// currentTip as fromRev; the merge commit's tree contains both the
700		// current-tip files and the incoming ResultRev files, so the diff
701		// against currentTip yields exactly the files affected by the merge.
702		// Unrelated local modifications are preserved.
703		_ = gitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead(wd, currentTip)
704	
705		result := &LandResult{
706			Status:            "landed",
707			NewTip:            mergeSHA,
708			Merged:            true,
709			MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
710		}
711		if req.EvidenceDir != "" {
712			landEvidence(wd, targetBranch, req, gitOps, result)
713		}
714		landPush(wd, targetBranch, result.NewTip, hasOrigin, gitOps, result)
715		return result, nil
716	}
717	
718	// landPush pushes the new target tip to origin when a remote exists. Push
719	// failure is non-fatal for the local land outcome; it is surfaced via
720	// PushFailed/PushError on the result.
721	//
722	// On the first push rejection landPush attempts one automatic recovery —
723	// fetch origin, merge our local tip into the new origin tip, advance the
724	// local target ref, and retry the push. The race-with-remote case (origin
725	// advanced with non-overlapping work between our local merge and our push)
726	// is the common failure in a multi-worker / multi-operator setup and can be
727	// fixed without operator intervention. If the auto-merge produces a conflict
728	// the loop cannot resolve, landPush marks the result PushConflict (a distinct
729	// signal from generic PushFailed) so the caller can park the bead for human
730	// review under a short cooldown rather than the long park used for
731	// non-recoverable push failures.
732	func landPush(wd, targetBranch, newTip string, hasOrigin bool, gitOps LandingGitOps, result *LandResult) {
733		if !hasOrigin {
734			return
735		}
736		firstErr := gitOps.PushFFOnly(wd, "origin", newTip, targetBranch)
737		if firstErr == nil {
738			return
739		}
740		// First push rejected. Try one automatic pull/merge/retry-push pass.
741		if recovered, mergeSHA, recErr := landPushAutoRecover(wd, targetBranch, newTip, gitOps); recovered {
742			result.NewTip = mergeSHA
743			result.PushRecovered = true
744			return
745		} else if recErr != nil && isPushAutoMergeConflict(recErr) {
746			// Auto-merge could not be resolved without operator input.
747			result.PushConflict = true
748			result.PushError = recErr.Error()
749			return
750		}
751		// Either recovery itself failed for non-conflict reasons (network, auth,
752		// fetch error, retry push still rejected) or origin was already at our
753		// tip. Surface the original push error so operator messages match the
754		// historical contract.
755		result.PushFailed = true
756		result.PushError = firstErr.Error()
757	}
758	
759	// landPushAutoMergeConflictError tags a recovery error that originates from
760	// an unresolvable git merge conflict, so landPush can branch on it without
761	// pattern-matching free-form error strings.
762	type landPushAutoMergeConflictError struct{ inner error }
763	
764	func (e *landPushAutoMergeConflictError) Error() string { return e.inner.Error() }
765	func (e *landPushAutoMergeConflictError) Unwrap() error { return e.inner }
766	
767	func isPushAutoMergeConflict(err error) bool {
768		for err != nil {
769			if _, ok := err.(*landPushAutoMergeConflictError); ok {
770				return true
771			}
772			type unwrapper interface{ Unwrap() error }
773			u, ok := err.(unwrapper)
774			if !ok {
775				return false
776			}
777			err = u.Unwrap()
778		}
779		return false
780	}
781	
782	// landPushAutoRecover attempts one fetch + merge + retry-push pass after the
783	// initial push was rejected. Returns recovered=true and the new merge SHA
784	// when the retry-push succeeds; otherwise returns recovered=false plus an
785	// error that indicates whether the recovery hit a merge conflict (wrapped in
786	// landPushAutoMergeConflictError) or some other failure.
787	//
788	// Caller responsibilities (already met by landPush):
789	//   - The local target ref is at newTip and not yet pushed.
790	//   - hasOrigin is true.
791	func landPushAutoRecover(wd, targetBranch, newTip string, gitOps LandingGitOps) (recovered bool, mergeSHA string, err error) {
792		if fetchErr := gitOps.FetchBranch(wd, "origin", targetBranch); fetchErr != nil {
793			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery fetch origin %s: %w", targetBranch, fetchErr)
794		}
795		originSHA, resolveErr := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, "refs/remotes/origin/"+targetBranch)
796		if resolveErr != nil {
797			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery resolve origin/%s: %w", targetBranch, resolveErr)
798		}
799		if originSHA == newTip {
800			// Origin is already at our tip — push must have failed for a reason
801			// other than a non-fast-forward (auth, branch protection, large
802			// blob, etc.). Recovery cannot help.
803			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("origin already at local tip; push failure not recoverable by auto-merge")
804		}
805		tempWT, mkErr := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-push-recover-*")
806		if mkErr != nil {
807			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery temp worktree: %w", mkErr)
808		}
809		_ = os.RemoveAll(tempWT)
810		if addErr := gitOps.AddWorktree(wd, tempWT, originSHA); addErr != nil {
811			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery add worktree at %s: %w", originSHA, addErr)
812		}
813		defer func() { _ = gitOps.RemoveWorktree(wd, tempWT) }()
814	
815		mergeMsg := fmt.Sprintf("Merge origin/%s into %s after push race", targetBranch, targetBranch)
816		if mergeErr := gitOps.MergeInto(tempWT, newTip, mergeMsg); mergeErr != nil {
817			return false, "", &landPushAutoMergeConflictError{inner: mergeErr}
818		}
819		mergeSHA, headErr := gitOps.HeadRevAt(tempWT)
820		if headErr != nil {
821			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery read merge HEAD: %w", headErr)
822		}
823		targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
824		if updErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, mergeSHA, newTip); updErr != nil {
825			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery advance %s to %s: %w", targetRef, mergeSHA, updErr)
826		}
827		_ = gitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead(wd, newTip)
828		if pushErr := gitOps.PushFFOnly(wd, "origin", mergeSHA, targetBranch); pushErr != nil {
829			return false, "", fmt.Errorf("auto-recovery retry push: %w", pushErr)
830		}
831		return true, mergeSHA, nil
832	}
833	
834	// landConflictAutoRecover attempts a 3-way merge of a preserved iteration ref
835	// onto the current target-branch tip using the ort strategy with -X ours. The
836	// strategy resolves mechanical conflicts (positional drift from parallel beads)
837	// by favouring the current tip's version of any conflicting sections while still
838	// including the preserved iteration's non-conflicting changes in the merge
839	// commit. If the merge succeeds the local target branch is advanced and the new
840	// merge commit SHA is returned.
841	//
842	// A non-nil error means the ort merge failed (unresolvable content conflict or
843	// git error) — the caller should escalate to a focused conflict-resolve agent.
844	// The target branch is never modified on error.
845	func landConflictAutoRecover(wd, preserveRef string, gitOps LandingGitOps) (string, error) {
846		targetBranch, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
847		if err != nil {
848			return "", fmt.Errorf("resolving target branch: %w", err)
849		}
850		targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
851	
852		currentTip, err := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, targetRef)
853		if err != nil {
854			return "", fmt.Errorf("resolving target tip %s: %w", targetRef, err)
855		}
856	
857		iterSHA, err := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, preserveRef)
858		if err != nil {
859			return "", fmt.Errorf("resolving preserved ref %s: %w", preserveRef, err)
860		}
861	
862		tempWT, mkErr := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-conflict-recover-*")
863		if mkErr != nil {
864			return "", fmt.Errorf("creating temp worktree: %w", mkErr)
865		}
866		_ = os.RemoveAll(tempWT)
867		if addErr := gitOps.AddWorktree(wd, tempWT, currentTip); addErr != nil {
868			return "", fmt.Errorf("adding temp worktree at %s: %w", currentTip, addErr)
869		}
870		defer func() { _ = gitOps.RemoveWorktree(wd, tempWT) }()
871	
872		mergeMsg := fmt.Sprintf("Merge preserved iteration %s after base drift (ort -X ours)", preserveRef)
873		out, mergeErr := internalgit.Command(
874			context.Background(), tempWT,
875			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
876			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",
877			"merge", "--no-ff", "-s", "ort", "-X", "ours", "-m", mergeMsg, iterSHA,
878		).CombinedOutput()
879		if mergeErr != nil {
880			_ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), tempWT, "merge", "--abort").Run()
881			return "", fmt.Errorf("ort merge: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), mergeErr)
882		}
883	
884		mergeSHA, headErr := gitOps.HeadRevAt(tempWT)
885		if headErr != nil {
886			return "", fmt.Errorf("reading merge HEAD: %w", headErr)
887		}
888		if updErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, mergeSHA, currentTip); updErr != nil {
889			return "", fmt.Errorf("advancing %s to %s: %w", targetRef, mergeSHA, updErr)
890		}
891		_ = gitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead(wd, currentTip)
892		return mergeSHA, nil
893	}
894	
895	// shortAttempt returns a 10-char slug derived from attemptID for use in temp
896	// branch names. When attemptID is empty, it returns the current timestamp.
897	func shortAttempt(attemptID string) string {
898		if attemptID != "" {
899			if len(attemptID) > 16 {
900				return attemptID[:16]
901			}
902			return attemptID
903		}
904		return NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405")
905	}

907	// ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult maps a LandResult onto an
908	// ExecuteBeadResult so the execute-bead loop sees the correct final status.
909	// It is the coordinator-pattern replacement for ApplyLandingToResult.
910	func ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult(res *ExecuteBeadResult, land *LandResult) {
911		if land == nil || res == nil {
912			return
913		}
914		switch land.Status {
915		case "landed":
916			// Fast-forward or merge commit — either way the target branch now
917			// contains the worker's result. ResultRev's own parent is still
918			// BaseRev so replay fidelity is preserved.
919			res.Outcome = "merged"
920			res.Reason = ""
921			res.PreserveRef = ""
922			if land.Merged {
923				res.Reason = "merged onto current tip"
924			}
925			if land.PushConflict {
926				res.Reason = PushConflictReasonPrefix + " " + land.PushError
927			} else if land.PushFailed {
928				res.Reason = PushFailedReasonPrefix + " " + land.PushError
929			}
930			// NewTip reflects the ref actually on the target branch (either
931			// ResultRev on the ff path or the merge commit SHA on the merge path).
932			if land.NewTip != "" {
933				res.ResultRev = land.NewTip
934			}
935		case "preserved":
936			res.Outcome = "preserved"
937			res.Reason = land.Reason
938			res.PreserveRef = land.PreserveRef
939		case "no-changes":
940			// Only overwrite when the worker itself did not already report
941			// a richer no-changes rationale.
942			if res.Outcome == "" || res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded {
943				res.Outcome = "no-changes"
944			}
945			if res.Reason == "" {
946				res.Reason = land.Reason
947			}
948		}
949		// Re-classify loop-visible status from the landing outcome.
950		reasonForStatus := res.Reason
951		if land.Status == "preserved" {
952			// Route preserve reasons through the land-conflict classifier so the
953			// loop sees land_conflict (not generic success).
954			reasonForStatus = "merge conflict"
955		}
956		res.Status = ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, reasonForStatus)
957		res.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, res.Reason, res.Error)
958	}
959	
960	// BuildLandRequestFromResult constructs a LandRequest for the coordinator from
961	// an ExecuteBeadResult. The coordinator always passes projectRoot as the
962	// workdir — the worker's original worktree has already been cleaned up by the
963	// time Land() runs.
964	func BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult) LandRequest {
965		return LandRequest{
966			WorktreeDir:  projectRoot,
967			BaseRev:      res.BaseRev,
968			ResultRev:    res.ResultRev,
969			BeadID:       res.BeadID,
970			AttemptID:    res.AttemptID,
971			TargetBranch: "",
972			EvidenceDir:  res.ExecutionDir,
973		}
974	}
